Section forward-references 4.2.2 (EXP-253) per AMENDMENT-001 separation of concerns. Word count ≈ 1754.
  docs/assets/eclipse-geometry-comparison.svg (~9 KB, 72 elements) — SVG 1, side-by-side globe-vs-dome shadow geometry schematic. Embedded in EXP-254 new_files[0].content.

  monitor/analyst/human-notes.json — both HNOTE-001 and AMENDMENT-001 consumed_progress.EXP_254 set to delivered.

Math verification (10 of 10 points, NASA-precise where applicable):
  1. Sun apparent diameter: USE NASA 0.5261° (semi 15'47.0"); operator's 0.5° was round-number approximation
  2. R_sun: CORRECTED from operator's 25 km to 26.3 km
  3. R_moon: CORRECTED from operator's 11 km to 12.0 km. NOTE: Aug 12 2026 is TOTAL eclipse, not annular as operator's draft stated (mag 1.0386 per NASA)
  4. UMBRA RADIUS FORMULA: operator's formula was wrong. Operator wrote (R_S-R_M)*h_moon/D = 11 km, claiming this as "shadow radius"; this is the DELTA, not the radius. CORRECTED to R_M - (R_S-R_M)*h_moon/D = 0.66 km umbra radius. Totality DIAMETER on disc: 1.3 km (not 22 km).
  5. Penumbra at ground: 42.4 km radius
  6. Lateral velocity 87 km/min: confirmed (sanity-check against ~38 km/min cos-latitude floor; argument robust)
  7. Single-observer totality: CORRECTED from operator's 15 sec to 0.9 sec (with 87 km/min) or up to 2 sec (with 38 km/min charity)
  8. Path width: PINNED to 294 km (NASA SE2026Aug12T)
  9. Totality duration: PINNED to 2m18.2s (138.2 sec), NOT operator's 2-5 min estimate
  10. Aetheric eclipse mechanism in V51: confirmed ABSENT in formula_runtime.json, model.html, live.html as of 2026-04-25. Trilemma option 3 stands as live alternative the dome has not taken.

Eclipse-type correction: operator's draft used "annular" / "path of annularity"; NASA confirms total (Feb 17 2026 was the annular; possibly source of operator's mix-up). Section uses "totality" / "path of totality" throughout.

Structural conclusion preserved despite numerical corrections:
  Path width ratio: real (294 km) / dome (1.3 km) ≈ 226× shorter on dome
  Duration ratio: real (138 sec) / dome (0.9 sec) ≈ 153× shorter on dome
  No path at all on dome — single stationary spot under moon's transit

Trilemma framing (geometric / borrowed / aetheric) preserved exactly. Dome's current implicit position named: option 2 (borrowed station selection) under option 1's parameters. Forward-references 4.2.4's tan(latitude) FSF identity to support the parasitic-station-selection finding.

Counterargument considered (per user preference): defender could read dome "Moon" as aetheric placeholder rather than physical occulter. This rescues the prediction by explicitly choosing option 3 (aetheric, no occultation), which immediately demands an explanation for station-specific localization without the moon's geometric shadow. Section commits to revisit if such a mechanism is published.

Kernel of truth: pre-registration practice (named stations, magnitudes with error bars, OpenTimestamps) is genuine and credited. Why kernel doesn't save claim: pre-registration commits the predictor to the prediction; it does not insulate the prediction from a geometric inconsistency baked into the model.

JSON validation: all four updated files (EXP-254, proposal, expansion-tracker, human-notes) pass JSON.parse. SVG validation: both files pass xml.etree.ElementTree.parse (eclipse-geometry-comparison.svg = 72 elements, eclipse-coverage-vs-time-hartland.svg = 75 elements).

Insertion-anchor dry-run: confirmed the substring-replacement insertion strategy works. After inserting at the (p4-426 close-tag, §4.2 outer-wrapper close-tag) boundary, <details> open/close count is balanced (12/12), p4-427 is nested correctly inside §4.2 wrapper, and p4-43-2 follows. Decider should not have ambiguity.

Live quote verification performed:
  - Both verbatim dome quotes confirmed live on https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html via curl (HTTP 200, ~1181 lines, 2026-04-25 ~21:00Z).
  - Quote 1 (Domain 11 falsification list): "Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within +/-3 nT of 0 (globe prediction)." — byte-identical to raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 104.
  - Quote 2 (eclipse countdown banner JS): "ECM predicts -[value] nT, globe predicts 0 nT. Requires Kp < 2." — byte-identical to raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 190.
  - Scope finding: both quotes are live.html-only; not present in raw-text/03-predictions.txt or other pages. Curmudgeon flagged to test whether broader prose framing ("the dome's eclipse argument depends on...") needs tightening to "the dome's live falsification dashboard depends on..." — current draft uses broader form because live.html IS the public scoreboard.

Self-falsification logic (in EXP-253 closing paragraph): BOU 2017 -10.9 nT is documented in dome's own raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 23 ("BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) | W004 empirical (-22.24 nT)") as one of two registered baselines for the V51 eclipse formula. By the dome's own published criterion (within +/-3 nT of 0 falsifies), -10.9 nT falsifies. The dome cannot consistently use BOU 2017 as input AND claim the globe predicts 0 nT.

Kernel of truth (per analyst directive — find what dome got right): the dome publishes an explicit numerical falsification criterion across all 14+ domains. Methodologically, this is rare and worth crediting — most pseudoscience refuses to publish anything testable. Why kernel doesn't save claim: a falsification criterion that compares your prediction to a misstated alternative is not a real test; the published number compares ECM to a fictional version of standard physics, not to the actual Chapman prediction (5-25 nT, sign-stable, shadow-tracking). Curmudgeon focus item 5 stress-tests this kernel.

Counterargument (per user preference): one could argue the dome's "globe prediction" wording is shorthand for "the globe predicts no SPECIFICALLY ECM-style aetheric signal" rather than "the globe predicts zero magnetic perturbation." Rebuttal: the published criterion is unconditional ("within +/-3 nT of 0") with no qualifier about mechanism. A reading where the dome means something other than what it published would require the dome to actually publish that other meaning. If the dome amends the criterion in response to our review, that itself becomes evidence (timestamped via OTS on status_history.json — see Timestamp Error tab) that the original was unsupported.
